channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: India's frustrated students find a symbol: the cockroach
item.description: Young Indians frustrated by unemployment and exam scandals are rallying behind an unusual symbol: the cockroach. NPR's Diaa Hadid reports from New Delhi.
item.content:encoded: Young Indians frustrated by unemployment and exam scandals are rallying behind an unusual symbol: the cockroach. NPR's Diaa Hadid reports from New Delhi.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: Election in Armenia becomes a test of Russian influence
item.description: Armenia is trying to move closer to Europe and the West, a move that's creating tension with Russia. Journalist Lucy Martirosyan reports from Yerevan ahead of an important election.
item.content:encoded: Armenia is trying to move closer to Europe and the West, a move that's creating tension with Russia. Journalist Lucy Martirosyan reports from Yerevan ahead of an important election.
item.dc:creator: Lucy Martirosyan

item.title: Israel has reportedly used white phosphorus near Lebanese cities and towns. What is it?
item.description: White phosphorus is not banned under international law, but can "create cruel injuries" and indiscriminate harm in civilian areas.
item.content:encoded:
White phosphorus is not banned under international law, but can "create cruel injuries" and indiscriminate harm in civilian areas.

(Image credit: Oliver Laban-Mattei)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Israeli airstrikes kill 9 including Lebanese army officers after ceasefire deal
item.description: The Israeli military confirmed hitting a vehicle and said the incident is being reviewed. Lebanon's President Joseph Aoun called the strike "a flagrant violation to Lebanese sovereignty and international law."
item.content:encoded:
The Israeli military confirmed hitting a vehicle and said the incident is being reviewed. Lebanon's President Joseph Aoun called the strike "a flagrant violation to Lebanese sovereignty and international law."

(Image credit: Hussein Malla)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Peru is set to elect its 10th president in a decade
item.description: Peruvians will elect their new president Sunday with polls suggesting a polarized but tight race between hard-right candidate Keiko Fujimori and leftist Roberto Sánchez.
item.content:encoded:
Peruvians will elect their new president Sunday with polls suggesting a polarized but tight race between hard-right candidate Keiko Fujimori and leftist Roberto Sánchez.

(Image credit: Rodrigo Abd)
item.dc:creator: Simeon Tegel

item.title: U.S. military says it shot down Iranian drones launched toward Gulf allies
item.description: The exchange of strikes comes as the Trump administration ramps up pressure on Iran to make a deal to end the conflict.
item.content:encoded:
The exchange of strikes comes as the Trump administration ramps up pressure on Iran to make a deal to end the conflict.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Putin promotes a new world economic order at St. Petersburg forum
item.description: At "Russian Davos," Putin ruled out meeting with Zelenskyy and promoted a new world economic order.
item.content:encoded: At "Russian Davos," Putin ruled out meeting with Zelenskyy and promoted a new world economic order.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: Xi Jinping will travel to North Korea next week in first visit since 2019
item.description: The announcement was made by both countries Friday a day after North Korea unveiled a new facility to produce nuclear fuel.
item.content:encoded:
The announcement was made by both countries Friday a day after North Korea unveiled a new facility to produce nuclear fuel.

(Image credit: Ahn Young-joon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: South Africa rolls out game-changing HIV shot amid funding shortfalls
item.description: A new twice-yearly HIV prevention injection could transform South Africa's fight against the epidemic — but U.S. aid cuts and limited doses threaten to slow its impact.
item.content:encoded:
A new twice-yearly HIV prevention injection could transform South Africa's fight against the epidemic — but U.S. aid cuts and limited doses threaten to slow its impact.

(Image credit: Ihsaan Haffe)
item.dc:creator: Kate Bartlett

item.title: China's highest bridge brings tourists and internet access to remote communities
item.description: A link to the highway that cuts travel times from hours to just minutes, and a symbol of a flow of investment that has provided unprecedented access to high-speed internet in this remote region.
item.content:encoded:
A link to the highway that cuts travel times from hours to just minutes, and a symbol of a flow of investment that has provided unprecedented access to high-speed internet in this remote region.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
